video_title: How Sui Is Building the Next Generation of Trading | Aslan Tashtanov, Mysten Labs | Sui Miami 2026
transcript: The Deepbook app is going to be very important here. It's starting with just BTC up down markets, the binary options, but we will be adding more markets. Imagine being able to trade the S&P 500 up down, but 24/7. This is not available anywhere. There are no options markets for the S&P 500 outside of regular trading hours. Options hasn't really worked in DeFi before, but Polymarket specifically has really figured out a way to, you know, get that to the retail users. And what they're going to be able to do is not only trade up down markets the way that they are used to right now, but we're also going to allow them to select any strike to trade against. USD Sui is going to be big. Gasless transactions. I think this one's going to be a game-changer because now all stablecoin transfers are going to be completely free. Not 0.0001 free. Your accounts do not even have to have any Sui in them for you to just be able to transfer them to any address. It's going to unlock the floodgates to all these institutions that can come in. I think we are building all of the pieces necessary to really win on-chain finance in the future. >> Hi everyone. Back at Sui Live in Miami, and we're sitting down with Aslan Tashkinov, DeFi Tech Lead, Deepbook and Mysten Labs. Aslan, thank you so much for joining us. How are you doing today? >> Thank you for having me. I am doing very good. Very excited to be here. Tons going on this this week, and it's just the energy's just so good. >> Yeah, you feel the energy. It feels like a an exciting event, and you know, we're here to talk to you about Deepbook. And our our Real Vision audience, we're very familiar with Deepbook and Sui already. But for those that are watching that may have not heard of Deepbook, can you can you tell us what that is? >> Yes. Deepbook is Sui's native liquidity layer. Originally started with Deepbook spot, right? The central limit order book that's fully on chain that is powering the Sui DeFi ecosystem. It is super easy to build on. It has the deepest and the best execution quality for trades on the major assets. And essentially, it is used by almost all of DeFi protocols to plug into whenever they need liquidity. >> Yeah, and DeepBook is such a, you know, it's it's such a big task to take, right? Instantly in a liquidity, you're dealing with liquidity. Why did you Why did you decide to build DeepBook? And then why And then to advance from that, why did you decide to build it on Sui? >> Okay. So, originally, I was on the market making side of things before I had joined Mysten, and I was very interested in markets, in trading. I've always been into that kind of stuff. And at some point, I was like, "Listen, I want to be on the consumer-facing side. I want to build products for consumers, for apps, for ecosystems, and I want to have, you know, build the best version of it." On-chain central limit order books have historically been very difficult to build, but Sui is the best blockchain for it because it is object-centric, which gives us a lot of parallelism and allows us to do a lot of transactions on DeepBook. Coming from our HFT background, that was very important. And it's also very fast, and it's also very cheap. All three of those are what you need to make a central limit order order book work very well at scale. >> Yeah, and and I love that you touched on that because I want to ask you a little bit because obviously you chose Sui, right? But like And you're building that central limit order book. But what does it unlock on on Sui that maybe wouldn't be available or or that opportunity wouldn't be there for you maybe on other chains? >> On Sui specifically, uh the unlock is really the public good aspect of it, right? We're able to build this uh protocol in a way that serves the entire DeFi ecosystem. We really see ourselves as DeFi infrastructure more than a protocol, if I'm being honest. So, it was important that we, you know, go to the chain where it is the easiest to build these things and these, uh, you know, protocols are composable in a very intuitive way. And Sui is just so easy to develop on. We have some teams that are building on DeepBook that are able to go from zero to one in just like two, three months and they're on mainnet launching their protocol. So, the ease of building on Sui, as well as all of the other technical benefits that Sui provides, is really the the key differentiator between Sui and the other L1s. >> Yeah, and you know, one of the things we've been one of the themes kind of at this conference is just the innovation on Sui and the speed at which they innovate and they're able to kind of adapt to the market, which is just truly impressive, honestly. And and and DeepBook is another example of that. So, I want to kind of ask you, how is your infrastructure evolved from when you first started to where we're where we're at today? >> Yeah, so we launched the central limit order book. That was three years ago, which is kind of crazy to think about, but it's already been three years since that's been live and it's been powering Sui DeFi since then. We launched margin. That's that was earlier this year. That was our native leverage layer. So, that allowed us to add leverage on top of DeepBook the spot exchange and now users can go long and short on, uh, spot, which is different than perps, right? So, we are kind of catering to this fully on-chain spot kind of markets. And now we are we just launched Predict, which is the third primitive that just launched three days ago, now. That one is a super exciting one. It is kind of, uh, it's really innovative and it's totally unique in the sense that, you know, the central limit order book and margin, those are primitives that have been around, right? People know about them. We didn't like invent something new there. Yes, we built it purposefully for Sweep, but we didn't really invent the concept. Predict is the first one where we're really taking DeFi native innovations like the AMM, like the prop AMM, or uh essentially all of the or or the shared liquidity aspect of it that's powering all the different markets. And those can only be done in DeFi, and that's one of the ways that we're innovating on that space, and Predict is not only going to power all the prediction layer stuff, but it's really going to be our Trojan horse towards options, which is something that is really the the longer-term vision with with this primitive. >> Yeah, I love that you mentioned that. And and honestly, Predict is a very interesting and unique product or, you know, introduction to the market. I want to talk to you kind of about the use how the user will interact with with that. Like, how are they basically going to see that and interact with that through the application? >> Okay, so that actually comes down to the second thing that we actually just announced earlier, the Deep Book app, which is our first app that's actually trader-facing. This is the first thing that we built for traders. Everything so far has been has been for builders, and we did build a very flourishing ecosystem around it. We have the friends, we have the market makers, we have the, you know, aggregators and the lending protocols that are all plugged in. And now for the very first time, we're building something for traders. And Predict is going to be the first experience that we're going to unlock with the Deep Book app. And users and traders are now going to be able to essentially start with Bitcoin price action. They're going to be able to predict whether Bitcoin is going to go up or down, but they're going to be able to do it in a very unique way that they cannot do anywhere else at the moment. Um Would you like me to like dive into all of the details? >> would, yes. >> Okay, okay. Um I don't know how much you guys know about, you know, prediction markets. I'm sure everybody knows about prediction markets, but specifically the Bitcoin up-down markets, okay? Uh the key with these markets is that these are actually options markets, okay? You're trading options contracts under the hood, which nobody is really thinking about it that way. Options hasn't really worked in DeFi before, but Polymarket specifically has really figured out a way to, you know, get that, you know, to the retail users in their hands. And that is very interesting and very unique, and we are really going to capitalize on that to get the initial, you know, retail user base. And what they're going to be able to do is not only trade up-down markets the way that they are used to right now, but we're also going to allow them to select any strike to trade against. So, right now, you can only trade on pre-made markets on other venues. Uh you cannot like create a market on demand. With Predict, the contract prices are computed on demand. That means every single market is generated on demand whenever you go into trade. That means uh at any given time, you can say, "Hey, I want to bet that the price of Bitcoin is going to go above $80,123." And that number can be just whatever you want that number to be, and that can be an input, and you're going to be you're going to be able to pay and and buy that contract. And if you are right, the payout is a dollar. If you are wrong, the payout is zero. Essentially, that's how it works. And within it with this new liquidity model that we've developed, that we've financially engineered, that by the way, we worked with a lot of like um you know, derivatives uh uh research teams as well as um risk curators to really make sure that this this model is robust. Um this model allows us to do a lot of cool, unique things. It allows us to do range bets. So, if you want to bet that BTC's going to be between 80 and 85K, that is a contract on its own, and that's going to be automatically available to you. And lastly, uh I mentioned margin earlier, margin, the thing that we're going to we added leverage to spot too. We're using the same margin pools to now add leverage to predictions, which is something that really hasn't been solved yet. So, we're solving continuous strikes, uh native range, and leverage all in one go. All three have not been solved. So, this is the first product to market that solves all three. So, I'm super excited about that. I think there I mean there's tons to go into there, but like that is a generally the the overview there. >> I I I'm almost I I almost want to use it like right now. Is it Is this available for users to to When will this be available? Is it available now, or how how can users kind of learn more about this? >> Right now, uh we launched testnet the primitive on testnet 3 days ago. And what I mean by the primitive is is the smart contracts along with the liquidity that power the protocol. We already have a lot of ecosystem builders that are integrating it as we speak, and a testnet version of the app is also going to be available within the next couple of weeks. So, that's what we're doing on testnet. Mainnet is coming soon. We still have a lot of things to iron out, but it's going to be very soon. Couple of months, not too long. It's going to be in the summer, okay? So, mainnet is coming in the summer, and when we launch, you're going to get this really really awesome new unique experience that really no other venue has. >> That's right. And then And then I love that you you went into depth with this, because that's exactly what I was speaking about with the the the way that the the sweet ecosystem adapts and innovates, and you just I love that you just gave us a real use case live example happening right now. And And I also feel like you kind of gave us a glimpse into the future of DeepBook and and kind of where this is all going. But I want to just ask you directly, like where do you see DeepBook's future in the next few years? >> Yes. Okay, the DeepBook app is going to be very important here. It's starting with just BTC up down markets, the binary options. But, we will be adding more markets. Imagine being able to trade the S&P 500 up down, but 24/7. This is not available anywhere. There are no options markets for the S&P 500 outside of regular trading hours. So, that's going to be very unique once we figure out how to do all those things, but we already are working on that, and that's going to be a a fast follow to the launch. Then, we have to remember I'm going to I'm going to bring it back to the options piece here, because yes, we're starting with these up down markets, but really the end goal is going to be longer dated options where institutions can come in and participate. Think of it this way. If we can figure out a way, or once we figure out a way where we get that retail flow and the retail trading activity to also impact the longer dated expirations, and allow institutions to trade along with retail within the same shared liquidity, we can enable unique things like, you know, having these BTC treasury companies go onto our platform, use their BTC as collateral, maybe even through Hashi, right? And write calls on that on that BTC and earn a premium. This is a new unique way that they can start, you know, earning extra APR on this on this asset that they're just holding, not really doing anything with, right? And um that is the ultimate vision. We've got to get it to a place where the the liquidity is deep, and we have longer expirations with a lot more participants. And once we get the institutionals in, we're going to create a whole new set of structured products around this thing that's really going to power not only just the, you know, short-term DJ trading activity, but also the long-term, you know, yield and passive yield and active yield and all these different strategies that you can do with with the underlying assets. >> You know, one thing that really impresses me with Sui cuz I you just mentioned Hashi as well is like the flywheel that just within this ecosystem, right? There you have Deep Book that can, you know, benefit with Hashi and I'm sure Beep and like all these other, you know, protocols and and services that are within the Sui ecosystem. They're all like working off of each other and I think that's what makes it such a such a powerful thing. >> You know, you you're you're totally right. And the thing with all these different pieces are it's when you're first building them, it's not immediately clear how they're going to be used in the end. When we first started Hashi, you know, over last year, right? It wasn't clear that oh, we're going to also build options and then we're going to combine it. >> Right. >> But we do know that if we have a central limit order book, if we have leverage, if we have some sort of options primitive, if we have Hashi somewhere else, wherever BTC play somewhere else, if we have USD Sui somewhere else, right? All of these things, there will be a way to combine them in unique ways. And we continuously discover these as we build and as we add these new components. So, you know, the the the thing here to take away is like we have to, you know, build the right primitives and the answers are going to start appearing after we've done it.
